data.title: Owens & Minor cancels Rotech buyout over regulatory issues
data.description:
Owens & Minor (NYSE:OMI) on Thursday announced an agreement to terminate its previously announced $1.36B acquisition of privately held home medical equipment provider Rotech Healthcare Holdings (ROTK) due to regulatory issues.

Concurrently, Glen Allen, Virginia-based healthcare solution provider announced plans to redeem $1B of notes issued in April to fund the acquisition. Other financing options, including term loan commitments, will also be terminated, the company said.

Owens & Minor (NYSE:OMI) has already paid $80M to Rotech under the terms of the all-cash merger deal, which the company previously expected to close by the end of 2024 in a bid to expand its footprint in the home-based care market.

Noting that the U.S. regulators have declined to clear the transaction, CEO Edward Pesicka said, “For many months, our teammates, along with the Rotech team, have worked tirelessly in cooperation with the Federal Trade Commission to close this transaction.”

“The path to obtain regulatory clearance for this merger proved unviable in terms of time, expense, and opportunity.”

data.title: Biggest stock movers Thursday: PVH, MDB, TOPS, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged lower on Thursday after initial jobless claims rose more than expected last week, fueling concerns about the labor market. Investors also remained cautious amid lingering uncertainty over the Trump administration’s trade policies, with focus shifting to Friday’s closely watched nonfarm payrolls report.

Here are some of Thursday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • MongoDB (NASDAQ:MDB) +15% – Shares rallied after the database company posted better-than-expected Q1 results, raised its full-year guidance, and expanded its share buyback program. For Q2, MongoDB expects revenue of $548M–$553M and adjusted EPS of $0.62–$0.66, above analyst estimates of $549.28M and $0.58, respectively. Full-year revenue is now forecast at $2.25B–$2.29B, up from $2.24B–$2.28B, while adjusted EPS is projected at $2.94–$3.12, significantly above the previous $2.44–$2.62 range and the $2.65 consensus.
  • Verint Systems (NASDAQ:VRNT) +21% – Shares surged after the company delivered better-than-expected Q1 results, highlighted by a 24% Y/Y increase in AI annual recurring revenue (AI ARR), which includes its AI-powered software offerings. Verint expects AI ARR to grow more than 20% for the full year, an acceleration from last year. The company is targeting 8% ARR growth overall, and with expanding cash contribution margins, it anticipates a 12% increase in free cash flow for the year.
  • Five Below (NASDAQ:FIVE) +5% – Shares gained after the specialty discount retailer reported a top- and bottom-line beat for Q1, with comparable sales rising 7.1%, ahead of the 6.4% consensus. The company opened 55 new stores during the quarter, bringing the total to 1,826 locations across 44 states, a 13.8% increase Y/Y. For Q2, Five Below guided net sales between $975M and $995M, ahead of the $958.3M consensus. Adjusted EPS is expected in the range of $0.50 to $0.62 (midpoint $0.56 vs. estimate $0.58), with guidance factoring in the impact of tariffs. The company expects 7% to 9% growth in comparable sales and plans to open approximately 30 net new stores. Separately, Five Below announced that CFO Kristy Chipman will step down for personal reasons. Current COO Ken Bull, who previously served as CFO for over a decade, will assume the finance chief duties on an interim basis.

Biggest stock losers

  • TOP Ships (NYSE:TOPS) -12% – Shares fell after a 44% rally in the previous session, as the company announced plans to spin off two Suezmax tanker vessels into a new publicly traded entity, Rubico. Rubico, currently a subsidiary of TOP Ships, will become an independent Nasdaq-listed company, holding the M/T Eco Malibu and M/T Eco West Coast—modern, high-spec, fuel-efficient tankers. As part of the spin-off, TOP Ships will distribute 100% of Rubico’s common shares to shareholders and warrant holders of record as of June 16, 2025. Rubico also expects to raise $1.5M via a private placement at $20 per share.
  • PVH Corp. (NYSE:PVH) -18 — Shares tumbled after the apparel maker slashed its full-year and Q2 earnings guidance due to tariff-related pressures, overshadowing stronger-than-expected Q1 results. For FY2025, PVH now expects non-GAAP EPS of $10.75 to $11.00, down from the prior range of $12.40 to $12.75. The new midpoint of $10.88 comes in below the consensus of $11.08. The revision reflects a $1.05 per share unmitigated impact from tariffs, partially offset by mitigation efforts and a $0.10 benefit from foreign exchange. For Q2, the company projects revenue growth in the low single digits Y/Y (flat to slightly up on a constant currency basis) and sees EPS in the range of $1.85 to $2.00, compared to $3.01 a year earlier and below the $2.06 consensus. The Q2 outlook includes a ~$0.20 per share hit from tariffs, with minimal FX impact expected.

data.title: Victoria's Secret announces new earnings date
data.description:
• Victoria’s Secret (NYSE:VSCO) will release first quarter results before the open on June 11 with its earnings call scheduled for 8:30am ET.
  • The company recently postponed its most recent quarterly results  due to a security incident that disrupted corporate and online operations on May 24. This prevented its staff from completing the financial results for the quarter.
  • For Q1, the company said it expects net sales to be down by less than 1% from last year to $1.35B versus prior expectations to be between $1.30B to $1.33B. This is expected to contribute to a profit of $0.09 per share versus the initial guidance of a loss of $0.10 to a profit of $0.10 per share. Adjusted operating income of $32M in Q1 also exceeds the original guidance of $10M to $30M.
  • Shares last traded 3% higher.

data.title: Redfin stockholders approve sale to Rocket Companies
data.description:
Redfin (NASDAQ:RDFN) stockholders approved the company's proposed sale to Rocket Companies (RKT), the company said on Thursday.

The shareholders also approved, on an advisory basis, the compensation that the company's named executive officers may receive in connection with the proposed sale.

Shares representing 74.1M were voted in favor of the merger, compared with 855,453 against and 73,982 abstaining.

In the compensation proposal, 71.4M shares were voted in favor, 2.90M against, and 742K abstained.

The closing of the transaction is expected to occur on or before July 1, 2025. 

Redfin (NASDAQ:RDFN) stock rose 1.5% in morning trading.

data.title: Trump and China's Xi hold phone call, details awaited
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping have held a phone call, CNN reported on Thursday, citing a person familiar with the matter. 

The White House did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha. The phone call was first reported by Chinese state media Xinhua, which added that the call was requested by Trump.  

U.S. stock index futures rose sharply on Xinhua's report, but quickly gave up those gains. Stocks have fluctuated after the opening bell, as traders await more details and clarity. 

Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500): (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

And here are some China-linked ETFs: (NYSEARCA:KWEB), (NASDAQ:PGJ), (NYSEARCA:CQQQ), (NYSEARCA:FXI), (NYSEARCA:GXC), (NASDAQ:MCHI), (NYSEARCA:FLCH), (BATS:CNYA), (NYSEARCA:ASHR), and (NYSEARCA:YINN).

data.title: The CPI print could be the key to answering the question of tariffs and inflation – GlobalData TS Lombard
data.description:
Tariffs imposed during the recent trade disputes have not yet appeared in official price data, creating a puzzling situation for economists, according to Dario Perkins, managing director of Global Macro at GlobalData TS Lombard.

As clients have asked, Perkins offered three potential explanations for this unexpected phenomenon.

The first explanation suggests foreign exporters might be absorbing the tariffs themselves, a claim made by some officials. However, he dismisses this theory, noting “the evidence doesn’t support that claim.”

Perkins added that, while “U.S. import prices from China have declined just 1%, the tariff rate is up 30 percentage points,” indicating exporters are not cutting their prices nearly enough to offset the additional customs charges.

A second possibility comes from a Federal Reserve study that found competing economic forces may be offsetting the tariffs’ inflationary effects. According to this study, the Fed found that “tariffs had raised U.S. consumer prices but that the effect was obscured by other prices, which had declined since the tariffs were introduced.”

However, Perkins questioned whether these opposing forces could balance each other so quickly, particularly noting how the “Liberation Day tariffs” might have accelerated deflationary pressures by threatening recession.

The most likely explanation, he said, involves delayed effects that will become more apparent over time. This theory explained that the tariff effect “has only been delayed and will emerge more clearly over the summer.”

He pointed to several contributing factors, including “delays in the transportation of goods from areas like China” and the fact that “U.S. companies accumulated inventories as a buffer against the near-term effects of tariff inflation.”

Perkins’s analysis concluded by advising close attention to upcoming price data, particularly the Consumer Price Index.

“It will be important to watch price data closely over the next few months to see if the impact of the tariffs becomes more visible,” he said. The CPI print “should capture tariff effects before they show up in other indices such as producer prices,” since “the CPI includes imports, the PPI doesn’t.”

More on U.S. economy and inflation:

  • Nasdaq, S&P, Dow look for direction as China President Xi Jinping and Trump hold call
  • Weak Jobs And Rising Inflation Fuel Stagflation Fears
  • U.S. trade deficit narrows sharply in April as imports plunge
  • Trump again urges Powell to cut rates after soft private sector job growth

data.title: Honey Badger Silver announces CFO appointment; shares up
data.description:
• Honey Badger Silver (OTCQB:HBEIF) has appointed Rob Suttie as chief financial officer, effective June 5, 2025.
  • Suttie brings nearly 30 years of financial reporting experience and is president of Marrelli Support Services.
  • HBEIF shares up nearly 7% on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Apple's EPS might take 2-3% hit after court upholds Epic injunction: analysts
data.description:
A federal appeals court decision to uphold an order forcing Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) to allow link-out payments from its App Store for purchases might not impact revenue as much as feared, but it could lead to earnings per share headwind of 2-3%, according to analysts.

Apple shares edged up 0.4% during early market action on Thursday.

"We do not expect all developers to move immediately out of the Apple ecosystem, and initial App Store data from May supports this thesis, but the June data will be the real test," said Evercore ISI analysts, led by Amit Daryanani, in an investor note. "We think some developers will choose to remain within the ecosystem despite the higher fees due to the convenience, trust, and frictionless experience Apple's billing system provides. We also think Apple has a strong argument to make to the appeal's court that the lower court's decision constitutes an unjust seizure of Apple's private property."

The appeals process could take up to two years or more. Despite the order taking effect on April 30, Evercore noted that App Store revenue increased 13% overall in May, and grew 10% in the U.S. market.  

"The App store generates ~$21B in sales annually," Evercore said. "As part of the ongoing AAPL vs. EPIC lawsuit, the judge ruled that Apple had to allow third-party transactions on iOS, and it was not able to charge a fee on those transactions. At risk is the ~$7B we estimate Apple earns from charging fees to US developers. Assuming that $7B fully went away, it would imply a 6% hit to EPS, but we think the actual impact will be smaller."

J.P. Morgan estimated the order could create a 2% to 3% headwind for Apple EPS.

"Although the denial of a stay is a headwind for Apple, we continue to believe the magnitude of impact is likely to be materially lower than currently being feared by investors," said J.P. Morgan analysts, led by Samik Chatterjee, in a Thursday note.

Meanwhile, Morgan Stanley, citing a recent survey it conducted on U.S. iPhone users, said 2% of Apple EPS is at risk. The survey found that 28% of respondents would be "Extremely Likely" to circumvent App Store payments.

Chatterjee said, "if real-world behavior mimicked our survey, 10% of App Store revenue, 3% of Services revenue, and 2% of Apple EPS would be 'at-risk'."

"Leveraging the data points from our latest AlphaWise survey, we estimate the revenue at risk from this injunction is ~$3.7B, assuming 28% of US App Store revenue would link-out (both in-app purchases and subscriptions) and Apple would collect a 0% take rate, equating to an EPS impact of 16c (or 2%) in a worst-case scenario," he added.

Evercore also noted that gaming constitutes the bulk, or 65% of U.S. App Store revenue, with many of these being one-time purchases of $0.99. If users go to alternative payment sites, such as Stripe, which charges 3% plus $0.30 per transaction, it would amount to a higher payment than the 27% charged by Apple. 

Apple highlighted that for more than 90% of billings and sales through the App Store in 2024, developers did not pay any commission.

"It's incredible to see so many developers design great apps, build successful businesses, and reach Apple users around the world," said Apple CEO Tim Cook.

data.title: Mobile-health Network Solutions announces share repurchase program of 214,000 shares
data.description:
• Mobile-health Network Solutions (NASDAQ:MNDR) announced on Thursday that its board of directors approved a share repurchase program to purchase up to 214,000 class A ordinary shares, effective immediately for up to one year from today.
  • Repurchases under the program may be made from time to time in open market transactions, in privately negotiated transactions, through block trades, or through other legally permissible means, said the company. 
  • The timing and actual number of shares repurchased will depend on a variety of factors, including price, market conditions, and corporate and regulatory requirements, it added. 
  • ZBH is +0.29% to $91.73.
  • Source: Press Release
